one_line_takeaway: GONE and NEDS extend LLM unlearning from flat sentence-level facts to structured knowledge-graph triples, showing that neighbor-anchored training (NEDS) achieves near-perfect direct/multi-hop forgetting while retaining substantially more locality (e.g., 0.839 on Mistral-7B) than NPO, ICU, AlphaEdit, or WISE baselines — but this is a machine-unlearning benchmark/methods paper, not an AI-security paper.
key_finding: On GONE/Wikidata with Llama-3-8B-Instruct (QA templates, Table 3), NEDS reaches 1.000 Direct UE, 1.000 Paraphrase UE, 0.725 Inverse UE, 1.000 Multi-hop UE, and 0.698 Locality with 0.000 Refusal Rate, versus NPO's 0.635 Direct / 0.595 Paraphrase / 0.209 Inverse / 0.406 Multi-hop / 0.938 Locality, and versus ICU's 1.000 Direct but only 0.273 Locality. On Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 (QA templates, Table 4), NEDS achieves 1.000 Direct, 1.000 Paraphrase, 0.562 Inverse, 0.999 Multi-hop, and 0.839 Locality — the paper's headline reported numbers (1.000 unlearning efficacy, 0.839 locality). The decision-boundary validation (Appendix B, n=254 forget probes / n=199 retain probes) shows NEDS raising ROC-AUC for forget-vs-retain separability from a pre-unlearning baseline of 0.448 to 0.660 (+0.212) with a log-probability gap of 0.079, compared to NPO's gap of 0.022 (paper states 'nearly 4x'). The neighbor-corruption ablation (Table 6, Llama-3-8B, n=400 probes) shows NEDS degrades gracefully: Direct UE stays roughly stable (0.062 clean vs 0.088 at 80% corruption) and Locality remains strong (0.882 clean vs 0.894 at 80% corruption), while Multi-hop UE rises from 0.223 to 0.355 at 80% corruption, consistent with weaker anchoring allowing more reasoning-chain leakage. On ConceptNet (commonsense, ablation), the paper reports these relations are harder to unlearn than Wikidata facts, with ICU achieving high FB Direct UE (1.000) but collapsing retention to 0.159%, while gradient methods (GA, NPO) barely move beyond the before-editing baseline.
core_contribution: The paper makes two own contributions, clearly distinguished from cited/related work: (1) GONE (Graph Oblivion and Node Erasure), the first knowledge-unlearning benchmark built from structured knowledge-graph (KG) triples (sampled from Wikidata for encyclopedic/functional relations and ConceptNet for commonsense/associative relations) rather than flat sentence-level facts, with probes generated by DeepSeek-V3 across Direct, Paraphrase, Inverse, Two-hop, Three-hop, and orthogonal Retain categories, and with a strict topological-orthogonality filter (schema disjointness + geodesic distance d(t,t')>3 + BFS-verified absence of latent inference paths) separating the Retain set from the Forget neighborhood; and (2) NEDS (Neighborhood-Expanded Distribution Shaping), a LoRA-based unlearning training method that suppresses the target fact via an NPO-style loss while anchoring k=10 mined correlated-neighbor probes with a cross-entropy 'anchor loss' to prevent collateral forgetting, formalized as a Lagrangian-relaxed constrained optimization with two propositions (local drift under unconstrained forgetting; anchoring as approximate null-space projection) and an empirical decision-boundary validation (ROC-AUC separability between forget/retain answer probabilities). The paper also introduces a new metric, Knowledge Connectivity Score (KCS), to measure whether unlearning is shallow (surface refusal) or deep (removal of the latent concept accessible via neighboring/multi-hop probes).
threat_model: Not applicable — this is a machine-unlearning/model-editing methods and evaluation paper, not a security or adversarial paper. There is no attacker, no adversarial capability assumption, and no security property being defended. The 'privacy/safety/IP' motivation for unlearning is mentioned only as one sentence of framing in the introduction (removing hazardous knowledge, copyrighted content) and is not developed into a threat model, attack, or defense evaluation anywhere in the paper.
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: GONE does not cover all knowledge dependencies, domains, or reasoning styles — it focuses on triplet-derived relations and omits more complex compositional or cross-domain reasoning; evaluation is limited to GONE, RWKU, and open-source models (Llama-3-8B-Instruct, Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2) and does not encompass all unlearning or model-editing methods. Additional grounded observations from the text: Inverse-relation unlearning remains substantially weaker than Direct/Paraphrase/Multi-hop for NEDS on both models (0.725 on Llama-3, 0.562 on Mistral, QA), indicating the anchoring boundary is less effective for bidirectional-link leakage than for other probe types; probe generation relies on a single LLM (DeepSeek-V3) as both generator and implicit filter, which is not independently validated for probe quality beyond a verbatim-leakage/ambiguity check; RWKU results are relegated to an appendix 'due to page limits' and are reported for context (Table 15 references RWKU's own published numbers) rather than as fully reproduced experiments in the main text reviewed here.

datasets_used: Wikidata (encyclopedic triples: capital_of, director, citizenship, performer, HQ location, producer, educated_at)
ConceptNet (commonsense triples: IsA, UsedFor, AtLocation, PartOf, CapableOf)
GONE benchmark (this paper's own construction, 212 entities, 1,614 forget probes per Table 5)
RWKU (Jin et al. 2024, used as a second evaluation benchmark, results reported in appendix)
quantitative_results: Llama-3-8B QA (Table 3): NEDS Direct UE 1.000, Paraphrase 1.000, Inverse 0.725, Multi-hop 1.000, Locality 0.698, Refusal 0.000
Llama-3-8B QA (Table 3): NPO Direct UE 0.635, Paraphrase 0.595, Inverse 0.209, Multi-hop 0.406, Locality 0.938
Llama-3-8B QA (Table 3): ICU Direct UE 1.000, Locality 0.273, Refusal 0.715
Mistral-7B QA (Table 4): NEDS Direct UE 1.000, Paraphrase 1.000, Inverse 0.562, Multi-hop 0.999, Locality 0.839, Refusal 0.000
Mistral-7B QA (Table 4): NPO Direct UE 0.992, Inverse 0.730, Multi-hop 0.998, Locality 0.717
Decision boundary (Appendix B, n=254 forget / n=199 retain): ROC-AUC before unlearning 0.448 -> NEDS 0.660 (+0.212); log-probability gap NEDS 0.079 vs NPO 0.022 (~4x)
Neighbor-corruption ablation (Table 6, Llama-3-8B, n=400): Direct UE 0.062 (0% corruption) to 0.088 (80% corruption); Locality 0.882 to 0.894; Multi-hop UE 0.223 to 0.355
baselines_compared: Negative Preference Optimization (NPO, Zhang et al. 2024b)
AlphaEdit (Fang et al. 2024, null-space constrained knowledge editing)
WISE (Wang et al. 2024, hypernetwork-based weight interpolation editing)
